chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.D-282 of 2026 (Faisal vs. Federation of Pakistan & Others) Date Order with the signature of the Judge 1. For orders on CMA No.991/2026 2. For orders on CMA No.992/2026 3. For the hearing of the main case 4. For orders on CMA No.993/2026. 12.02.2026. Mr. Achar Khan Gabol, Advocate for the Petitioner ***************** 1. Granted. 2to4. Through this petition, the Petitioner’s main grievance is that his appeal is pending before the Federal Service Tribunal, and due to the vacant posts with no Judges appointed to the Tribunal, he seeks suspension of the office order transferring him. In this regard, the Petitioner also placed on record that he had previously withdrawn one petition on the ground that he would approach the Federal Service Tribunal. Perusal of the record reveals that on 15.10.2025, the Petitioner did not press his petition. The Federal Service Tribunal, according to the Petitioner’s reliance at page-51, Annexure-B1, had no appointments after 07.11.2024. On this aspect, the Petitioner himself chose not to press the petition. Furthermore, the Petitioner filed an appeal before the concerned department; however, such an appeal was filed prior to the dismissal of his first petition. The contention that the Federal Service Tribunal is now vacant and has no knowledge of the matter is, therefore, misconceived. In view of the above, this petition is devoid of merit and is hereby dismissed in limine, along with pending applications, accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE
